def: The state of a woman bringing forth a child, or being in labor;parturition.
Having broad scutella on the front, and small scales on theposterior side, of the tarsus; -- said of certain birds.
Growing by the side of a petal, as a stamen.
Of or pertaining to the Hellenes, or Greeks.

Eating or feeding on flesh. The term is applied: (a) to animalswhich naturally seek flesh for food, as the tiger, dog, etc.; (b) toplants which are supposed to absorb animal food; (c) to substanceswhich destroy animal tissue, as caustics.
Something especially hated or dreaded; a bugbear.
An alembic; a still. [Obs.] Spenser. Shak.
The quality of turning; instability; tergiversation. [Obs.] SirP. Sidney.
Having the power to make propitious; pertaining to, or employedin, propitiation; expiatory; as, a propitiatory sacrifice. Sharp.
